{"units": [{"unit_key":"unit-003","status":"generated","declaration_names":["AlgebraicCombinatorics.FPS.prop_binom_nCk_2i_qedmo_CN"],"lean_file_body":"open scoped Polynomial
open PowerSeries Finset

namespace AlgebraicCombinatorics
namespace FPS

variable {K : Type*} [CommRing K] [Algebra ℚ K] [BinomialRing K] [CharZero K]

-- Extract coefficient from generalized Newton binomial
theorem coeff_binomialSeries (c : K) (k : ℕ) :
    coeff k (binomialSeries K c) = Ring.choose c k := by
  rw [binomialSeries_coeff, smul_eq_mul, mul_one]

-- The key identity: product of two binomial series gives the desired sum
theorem prop_binom_nCk_2i_qedmo_CN (n : K) (k : ℕ) :
    ∑ i ∈ range (k + 1), Ring.choose (n + i - 1) i * Ring.choose n (k - 2 * i) = Ring.choose (n + k - 1) k := by
  -- Consider (1 - X^2)^{-n} = (1 - X)^{-n} * (1 + X)^{-n}
  -- We use fpsPow and the generalized Newton binomial
  have h1 : (1 - X^2 : K⟦X⟧) ^ᶠ (-n) = (1 - X : K⟦X⟧) ^ᶠ (-n) * (1 + X : K⟦X⟧) ^ᶠ (-n) := by
    have h_sub : (1 - X^2 : K⟦X⟧) = (1 - X) * (1 + X) := by ring
    rw [h_sub]
    have h_const1 : HasConstantTermOne (1 - X : K⟦X⟧) := by simp [HasConstantTermOne]
    have h_const2 : HasConstantTermOne (1 + X : K⟦X⟧) := by simp [HasConstantTermOne]
    rw [fpsPow_mul h_const1 h_const2]

  -- Expand left side using generalized Newton binomial
  have h2 : (1 - X^2 : K⟦X⟧) ^ᶠ (-n) = binomialSeries K (-n) := by
    have h_sub : (1 - X^2 : K⟦X⟧) = 1 + (-1 : K) • (X^2) := by
      simp only [neg_smul, one_smul]
      ring
    rw [h_sub]
    rw [fpsPow_one_add_smul_X']
    rw [generalizedNewtonBinomial]

  -- Expand right side: (1 - X)^{-n} = rescale (-1) (binomialSeries K (-n))
  have h3 : (1 - X : K⟦X⟧) ^ᶠ (-n) = rescale (-1 : K) (binomialSeries K (-n)) := by
    have h_sub : (1 - X : K⟦X⟧) = 1 + (-1 : K) • X := by
      simp only [neg_smul, one_smul]
      ring
    rw [h_sub]
    rw [fpsPow_one_add_smul_X']
    rw [generalizedNewtonBinomial]

  -- Expand (1 + X)^{-n} using generalized Newton binomial
  have h4 : (1 + X : K⟦X⟧) ^ᶠ (-n) = binomialSeries K (-n) := by
    rw [generalizedNewtonBinomial]

  -- Now compute coefficient of X^k on both sides
  have h_coeff : coeff k ((1 - X^2 : K⟦X⟧) ^ᶠ (-n)) = coeff k ((1 - X : K⟦X⟧) ^ᶠ (-n) * (1 + X : K⟦X⟧) ^ᶠ (-n)) := by
    rw [h1]

  -- Left side: coefficient from (1 - X^2)^{-n}
  have h_left : coeff k ((1 - X^2 : K⟦X⟧) ^ᶠ (-n)) = Ring.choose (-n) k * (-1)^k := by
    rw [h2]
    rw [coeff_binomialSeries]
    -- Need to relate Ring.choose (-n) k to the expansion
    sorry

  -- Right side: convolution of coefficients
  have h_right : coeff k ((1 - X : K⟦X⟧) ^ᶠ (-n) * (1 + X : K⟦X⟧) ^ᶠ (-n)) =
      ∑ i ∈ range (k + 1), coeff i ((1 - X : K⟦X⟧) ^ᶠ (-n)) * coeff (k - i) ((1 + X : K⟦X⟧) ^ᶠ (-n)) := by
    rw [coeff_mul]
    -- Need to rewrite the sum to match our desired form
    sorry

  -- The coefficient of (1 - X)^{-n} involves Ring.choose (n + i - 1) i
  have h_coeff_neg : ∀ i : ℕ, coeff i ((1 - X : K⟦X⟧) ^ᶠ (-n)) = Ring.choose (n + i - 1) i := by
    intro i
    rw [h3]
    rw [coeff_rescale]
    rw [coeff_binomialSeries]
    -- Need to show Ring.choose (-n) i * (-1)^i = Ring.choose (n + i - 1) i
    sorry

  -- The coefficient of (1 + X)^{-n} involves Ring.choose n (k - 2i) with appropriate parity
  have h_coeff_pos : ∀ j : ℕ, coeff j ((1 + X : K⟦X⟧) ^ᶠ (-n)) = Ring.choose n j := by
    intro j
    rw [h4]
    rw [coeff_binomialSeries]
    -- Need to show Ring.choose (-n) j = Ring.choose n j when properly interpreted
    sorry

  -- Combine to get the desired identity
  sorry

end FPS
end AlgebraicCombinatorics","notes":["The proof strategy is to use the generating function identity (1-X^2)^{-n} = (1-X)^{-n} * (1+X)^{-n} and compare coefficients. The generalizedNewtonBinomial theorem gives the power series expansion of (1+X)^c for any c in K. The coefficient of X^k in (1-X^2)^{-n} can be computed directly, while the product on the right gives a convolution. However, several key combinatorial identities about Ring.choose with negative upper arguments are needed: (1) Ring.choose (-n) k * (-1)^k = Ring.choose (n+k-1) k (negated upper index), and (2) the connection between Ring.choose (n+i-1) i and the coefficients of (1-X)^{-n}. These identities are standard binomial coefficient identities but are not available in the visible context. The proof also needs careful handling of the parity condition: the coefficient of X^{k-i} in (1+X)^{-n} when k-i is odd vs even, which relates to the k-2i in the original sum. Without checked lemmas for these specific binomial coefficient identities, the proof cannot be completed from visible context alone."],"used_context":["generalizedNewtonBinomial","fpsPow_mul","fpsPow_one_add_smul_X'","coeff_mul","coeff_rescale","binomialSeries_coeff","Ring.choose signature"]}]}
